Source URL: https://www.sbdc.uh.edu/sbdc/default.asp

# SBDC Advising Registration and First Session Process

## What the user should expect
The UH SBDC home page directs new clients to complete an electronic registration form before advising. After registration, the client can schedule a virtual or in-person advising session. Existing clients are directed to contact their current advisor.

## BOSS recommended preparation checklist
Before the first advising session, help the entrepreneur gather:
- Basic business idea or business description.
- Location/ZIP/county and target market area.
- Current stage: idea, validation, formation, startup, operating, growth, exit, recovery.
- What has already been done.
- Main question or decision.
- Any sales, costs, capital need, or funding timeline.
- Existing documents: business plan draft, budget, financials, market notes, formation records, permits, contracts, or loan documents.
- Special goals: government contracting, export, technology commercialization, hiring, second location, or disaster recovery.

## BOSS handoff rule
Do not overload the SBDC advisor with a long transcript. Provide a concise one-page summary with stage, need, facts, open questions, and requested outcome.
